steps.title: Recognize what the passage focuses on
steps.content: The strongest clue is how the passage is organized: it notes that Davis and Evans's adaptation “bears little resemblance to the 1935 original,” then immediately explains how—through omitting parts and making brief gestures rather than exact duplication—before concluding that this approach led to an enduring album. The passage's entire structure is about what they changed.

steps.title: Confirm the transformative purpose
steps.content: Choice B directly captures this: describing how two artists transformed another artist's work. The passage does exactly this by showing the specific modifications Davis and Evans made and their successful result. The other three either focus on the wrong scope, make unsupported generalizations about critics and tradition, or claim a superiority argument the text never advances. That matches choice B — B is correct.
